metadata.form_title: Schedule K — Shareholders' Pro Rata Share Items (Form 1120-S)

facts.label: Ordinary business income (loss) — from Page 1 Line 21
Net rental real estate income (loss) — from Form 8825
Net short-term capital gain (loss) — from Schedule D
Net long-term capital gain (loss) — from Schedule D
Net §1231 gain (loss) — from Form 4797 Part I
§199A — Specified service trade or business (SSTB) indicator

rules.title: Ordinary business income passthrough
rules.description: Page 1 Line 21 flows directly to Schedule K Line 1. No modification.

authorities.relevance_note: Schedule K Line 1 — ordinary income from page 1 line 21
authorities.title: Instructions for Form 1120-S — U.S. Income Tax Return for an S Corporation

rules.title: Rental real estate from Form 8825
rules.description: Net income/loss from Form 8825 flows to K Line 2.

authorities.relevance_note: Schedule K Line 2 — rental real estate from Form 8825
authorities.title: Instructions for Form 1120-S — U.S. Income Tax Return for an S Corporation

rules.title: Capital gains routing
rules.description: Net STCG → K Line 7, Net LTCG → K Line 8a. From Schedule D.
rules.formula: net_short_term_capital_gain → K Line 7; net_long_term_capital_gain → K Line 8a

authorities.relevance_note: Schedule K Lines 7-8 — capital gains from Schedule D
authorities.title: Instructions for Form 1120-S — U.S. Income Tax Return for an S Corporation
Instructions for Schedule D (Form 1120-S) — Capital Gains and Losses and Built-in Gains

rules.title: Section 1231 gain routing
rules.description: Net §1231 gain from 4797 Part I → K Line 9. EXCESS gain after recapture. Ordinary recapture goes to Page 1 Line 4.

authorities.relevance_note: Schedule K Line 9 — §1231 gain from Form 4797
authorities.title: Instructions for Form 1120-S — U.S. Income Tax Return for an S Corporation

rules.title: Section 179 routing — separately stated
rules.description: §179 deduction from Form 4562 → K Line 11. NOT Page 1. This is a separately stated item that passes through to shareholders. Common error: §179 should NOT reduce ordinary income on Page 1.

authorities.title: IRC §179 — Election to Expense Certain Depreciable Business Assets
Instructions for Form 1120-S — U.S. Income Tax Return for an S Corporation
Instructions for Form 4562 — Depreciation and Amortization

rules.title: Charitable contributions — separately stated
rules.description: Charitable contributions → K Line 12a (cash) and 12b (noncash). Separately stated, NOT deducted on Page 1.

authorities.relevance_note: §1363(b)(2) — charitable not deducted at entity level
authorities.title: IRC §1363 — Effect of Election on Corporation
Instructions for Form 1120-S — U.S. Income Tax Return for an S Corporation
IRC §170 — Charitable Contributions

rules.title: QBI information routing
rules.description: §199A QBI items flow through K Line 17. Includes ordinary income, W-2 wages, UBIA of qualified property, SSTB indicator.
rules.formula: qbi_ordinary_income, qbi_w2_wages, qbi_ubia, qbi_sstb_indicator → K Line 17

authorities.relevance_note: §199A QBI deduction — items reported on K Line 17
authorities.title: IRC §199A — Qualified Business Income Deduction
Instructions for Form 1120-S — U.S. Income Tax Return for an S Corporation

rules.title: Pro rata allocation base
rules.description: Each K line item is allocated to shareholders based on pro rata share (daily ownership percentage). Unlike partnerships, S-Corps cannot do special allocations — must be pro rata per share per day.

authorities.title: IRC §1366 — Pass-Through of Items to Shareholders

authorities.relevance_note: §1377(a) — per share per day allocation rule
authorities.title: IRC §1377 — Definitions and Special Rule

rules.title: K Line 1 source: Page 1 Line 21
rules.description: Schedule K Line 1 = Page 1 Line 21 (ordinary business income/loss). This is the direct flow of the S-Corp's operating result to Schedule K for shareholder allocation.

authorities.title: Instructions for Form 1120-S (2025) — Full Document (fetched 2026-03-18)

rules.title: K Line 4 = Interest income
rules.description: Schedule K Line 4 is INTEREST INCOME (portfolio income). This is NOT Section 1231 gain. Interest income is separately stated because it is portfolio income under IRC 469.
rules.notes: IMPORTANT: K4 is NOT Section 1231. K9 is Section 1231. This was a source of confusion.

authorities.title: Instructions for Form 1120-S (2025) — Full Document (fetched 2026-03-18)

rules.title: K Line 5a/5b = Dividends
rules.description: K Line 5a = ordinary dividends, K Line 5b = qualified dividends (subset of 5a). Portfolio income, separately stated. NOT capital gains.

authorities.title: Instructions for Form 1120-S (2025) — Full Document (fetched 2026-03-18)

rules.title: K Line 7 source: Schedule D Part I Line 5
rules.description: Schedule K Line 7 = net short-term capital gain (loss) from Schedule D Part I Line 5. This comes from Form 8949 capital asset transactions, NOT from Form 4797.

authorities.relevance_note: Schedule D Part I Line 5 -> K7
authorities.title: Instructions for Schedule D (Form 1120-S) (2025) — fetched 2026-03-18

rules.title: K Line 8a source: Schedule D Part II Line 12
rules.description: Schedule K Line 8a = net long-term capital gain (loss) from Schedule D Part II Line 12. From Form 8949 capital asset transactions, NOT from Form 4797.

authorities.relevance_note: Schedule D Part II Line 12 -> K8a
authorities.title: Instructions for Schedule D (Form 1120-S) (2025) — fetched 2026-03-18

rules.title: K Line 9 source: Form 4797 Part I Line 7
rules.description: Schedule K Line 9 = net section 1231 gain (loss) from Form 4797 Part I Line 7. Section 1231 transactions BYPASS Schedule D on the 1120-S and flow directly to K Line 9. This is a key architectural difference from the 1040.
rules.notes: VERIFIED: 4797 Part I -> K9 directly. Does NOT go through Schedule D on 1120-S.

authorities.title: Instructions for Form 1120-S (2025) — Full Document (fetched 2026-03-18)
Instructions for Form 4797 (2025) — Sales of Business Property

rules.title: K Line 16c = Nondeductible meals
rules.description: K Line 16c (nondeductible expenses) includes the 50% nondeductible portion of meals. This amount also flows to M-1 Line 3b as an add-back and to M-2 as a reduction to AAA.

authorities.title: Instructions for Form 1120-S (2025) — Full Document (fetched 2026-03-18)

rules.title: K Line 16d = Total distributions
rules.description: K Line 16d = total distributions (property and cash) to shareholders. This amount flows to M-2 Line 7 and to each shareholder's K-1.

authorities.title: Instructions for Form 1120-S (2025) — Full Document (fetched 2026-03-18)
IRC §1368 — Distributions

rules.title: K Line 18 = Income/loss reconciliation
rules.description: Schedule K Line 18 is a reconciliation total. It should equal Page 1 Line 21. This is the same number that M-1 Line 7/8 must also equal.

authorities.title: Instructions for Form 1120-S (2025) — Full Document (fetched 2026-03-18)

line_map.description: Other information — §199A QBI items, investment income/expenses

line_map.notes: Informational total. Each line item flows separately to K-1.

line_map.description: K Line 1 -> K-1 Box 1: Ordinary business income (loss)
K Line 2 -> K-1 Box 2: Net rental real estate income (loss)
K Line 3c -> K-1 Box 3: Other net rental income (loss)
K Line 4 -> K-1 Box 4: Interest income
K Line 7 -> K-1 Box 7: Net short-term capital gain (loss)
K Line 8a/8b/8c -> K-1 Box 8a/8b/8c: Long-term capital gain, collectibles, unrec 1250
K Line 9 -> K-1 Box 9: Net section 1231 gain (loss)
K Line 10 -> K-1 Box 10: Other income (loss)
K Line 11 -> K-1 Box 11: Section 179 deduction
K Line 16a-f -> K-1 Box 16: Items affecting shareholder basis
K Line 17a-d -> K-1 Box 17: Other information (QBI, etc.)

diagnostics.title: §179 on Page 1
diagnostics.condition: section_179_deduction > 0 AND ordinary_business_income appears reduced by §179
diagnostics.message: §179 deduction should NOT reduce ordinary income on Page 1. It must flow to Schedule K Line 11 as a separately stated item.

diagnostics.title: Charitable on Page 1
diagnostics.message: Charitable contributions are separately stated items. They should NOT be deducted on Page 1 — they flow to K Line 12a/12b.

diagnostics.title: Capital gain/loss not separately stated
diagnostics.message: Capital gains and losses must be separately stated on K Lines 7-8. They should not be included in ordinary business income (K Line 1).

diagnostics.title: Missing QBI information
diagnostics.message: Ordinary business income exists but no §199A QBI information on Line 17. Most S-Corps must report QBI items for the shareholder's §199A computation.

diagnostics.title: Section 1231 double-count check
diagnostics.condition: K4 contains section_1231_gain AND K9 contains section_1231_gain
diagnostics.message: Section 1231 gain appears on both K Line 4 (interest income) and K Line 9 (net section 1231). K Line 4 is for INTEREST INCOME, not Section 1231. Verify that 1231 gain is only on K Line 9.

diagnostics.title: Page 1 Line 4 source verification
diagnostics.message: Page 1 Line 4 does not match Form 4797 Part II Line 17. Line 4 should equal the ordinary gain/loss from 4797 Part II, NOT the Section 1231 gain from Part I.

diagnostics.title: K18 does not equal Page 1 Line 21
diagnostics.message: Schedule K Line 18 (income/loss reconciliation) does not equal Page 1 Line 21. These must match.

diagnostics.title: Schedule D Section 1231 contamination
diagnostics.message: Section 1231 amounts appear on Schedule D. On the 1120-S, Section 1231 goes directly to K Line 9 from Form 4797 Part I Line 7, NOT through Schedule D.

diagnostics.title: Schedule D totals vs 8949
diagnostics.message: Schedule D totals do not match Form 8949 category totals. Verify all 8949 transactions are correctly categorized (A-F) and totaled on Schedule D.

tests.notes: §179 is separate from ordinary income. K Line 1=200K, K Line 11=50K.

tests.scenario_name: S-Corp with capital gains and recapture
tests.notes: Each item flows to its own K line — nothing combined.
